mcqs.question: Which operation inserts an element onto the top of a stack?
mcqs.explanation: Push is the operation that inserts (adds) an element onto the top of a stack.

mcqs.question: What does the Pop operation return?

options.text: The bottom element of the stack
The top element of the stack and removes it
The top element without removing it

mcqs.explanation: Pop removes and returns the top element of the stack, following the LIFO (Last In First Out) principle.

mcqs.question: What condition indicates stack underflow?
mcqs.explanation: Stack underflow occurs when a Pop operation is attempted on an empty stack. When top equals -1, the stack is empty and any Pop will cause underflow.

mcqs.question: What is the condition for stack overflow in an array-based implementation?
mcqs.explanation: Stack overflow occurs when a Push operation is attempted on a full stack. When top equals MAX_SIZE - 1, the array is full and no more elements can be pushed.

mcqs.question: What is the time complexity of Push and Pop operations on a stack?
mcqs.explanation: Both Push and Pop are O(1) operations because they only involve adding or removing an element at the top of the stack, which requires constant time.
